raw_consumer_statement:
I was reviewing my accounts on XXXX. XXXX XXXX  when I noticed an interest charge that I was previously unaware of. Upon further review they had been charging me interest on a charge that had been put onto my account back in XX/XX/year>. Apparently this purchase was placed on my account due to a XXXX  returned check. They had already credited the account even though the check had not cleared through, therefore charged back the account in the amount that was credited. I had opened the account taking advantage of a promotional XXXX XXXX on balance transfers. When I called they said that was standard practice instead of just reversing the credit they charged the credit card superseding any promotional rate thereby now charging me for a purchase under standard interest rates. 
This is the first time I have ever had a returned check on a promotional offer and was unaware of practice. I reviewed their charges and fees disclosures page and saw no mention that any returned check payment credit would be charged as a standard purchase subject to standard interest rates. I requested a full terms and agreement and no where does it say I would be charged a standard purchase that would supersede any promotional rate. I had made a subsequent payment to the account to avoid late fees so my account was still in good standing at the time of the error. I had not made any purchases or used the card in anyway to avoid the standard interest until the card was paid off. I had the call upgraded to a supervisor level and they refused any type of consideration except to say it's in their full terms and disclosures and offered to send me a copy. This seems completely unethical and definitely not consumer friendly to one who has been trying hard to reverse bad credit decisions in the past and trying to save some money to pay off debts without going through XXXX  or other " credit '' repair companies.
